content.problemStatement: Sereja have **N** programs. Sereja should run every program on two computers. Program number **i** works **A[i]** seconds on first computer and **B[i]** seconds on second. On each computer Sereja isn't able to run two programs in parallel, also Sereja isn't able to run one program on two computers in one moment. Sereja need to run all programs on both computers in minimal time. Help Sereja in his problem.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of programs. Then, N lines follow, each containing two integers A[i] and B[i], the time it takes for the i-th program to run on the first and second computers, respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum total time to run all programs on both computers, followed by the order in which the programs should be run on each computer.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there is only one program, so it runs on both computers. For the second test case, the programs are assigned to the computers based on their running times.
The programs are assigned to the computers based on their running times.
The programs are assigned to the computers based on their running times.

solution.approach: The key insight is to sort the programs based on the difference between their running times on the two computers. Then, we assign the programs to the computers in an alternating manner, starting with the program that has the largest difference in running times. This approach ensures that the total time to run all programs on both computers is minimized.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total. The sorting step takes O(N log N) time, and we do this for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the running times of the N programs.
